hook: $47 made my ruined couch look brand new

aida_breakdown.attention: Extreme close-up of a dog on a pet-hair-covered, stained couch. Dollar amount hits on screen in under two seconds.
aida_breakdown.interest: Wide shot of the full disaster sofa — sagging, visibly gross, noticeably avoided. Creator admits guests won't sit on it. Lint roller attempt fails in real time.
aida_breakdown.desire: Hands stretch the waterproof cover taut over every corner in three minutes flat. Reveal: same sofa, now looks like a furniture showroom piece. Creator drops the skeptic-to-convert line.
aida_breakdown.action: Amazon listing on phone screen with price visible. Single CTA delivered verbally and on screen.

value_equation.dream_outcome: Sofa looks like a $500 furniture-store display — no pet hair, no stains, taut and modern
value_equation.perceived_likelihood: Specific $47 price point plus 'I was skeptical, I was wrong' framing signals real-person credibility, not ad copy
value_equation.effort_sacrifice: No tools, no sewing, no landlord permission — stretches on by hand over any sofa size

scenes.visual_prompt: Extreme close-up of a golden retriever sitting on a visibly stained, pet-hair-covered beige couch. Hair clings to every cushion. Warm natural living room light.
scenes.voiceover: This $47 cover saved my couch from my dog.

scenes.visual_prompt: Wide shot of a lived-in living room. The full sofa is visible — sagging, stained, covered in pet hair. Obvious empty space where guests normally sit.
scenes.voiceover: I was embarrassed every time someone came over.

scenes.visual_prompt: Close-up of frustrated hands pulling a lint roller across the couch, lifting it to show it completely covered in dog hair after a single stroke, then tossing it aside in defeat.
scenes.voiceover: I tried everything. Nothing worked for more than a day.

scenes.visual_prompt: Hands stretching a dark charcoal slipcover over a couch arm and corner, snapping it taut. The cover pulls smooth with no bunching. A subtle timer graphic reads '3 min'.
scenes.voiceover: I was skeptical. I was wrong. On in three minutes.

scenes.visual_prompt: Dramatic full reveal: the same couch now covered in a taut, modern charcoal stretch slipcover. Clean lines, zero wrinkles, looks like a furniture showroom display. The dog walks by and settles onto it.
scenes.voiceover: Guests actually complimented my couch. The same couch.

scenes.visual_prompt: Phone screen showing the Amazon product listing for the Mamma Mia waterproof stretch sofa cover with the $47 price clearly visible. Clean white product background.
scenes.voiceover: Everything linked in bio. Forty-seven dollars.

caption:
$47 made my ruined couch look brand new — and I was not expecting that.

My dog destroyed it. Guests were visibly avoiding sitting on it. I ordered this Mamma Mia waterproof stretch cover completely skeptical it would do anything useful.

It went on in 3 minutes. No tools. No help. And my sofa looks like it came from a furniture store.

Waterproof, machine washable, fits sectionals and standard sofas. Link in bio.

#couchcover #sofacover #petfurniture #springcleaning #dogmom

affiliate_strategy.primary_product: Mamma Mia waterproof stretch sofa cover
